draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a persistent left sidebar for core workspace functions paired with horizontal tabs for context-switching within sections
facade.axisTargeted: Navigation structure (sidebar vs tabs vs hybrid)
facade.label: Sidebar with tabs
facade.acceptImplies: Explore vertical hierarchies, persistent navigation, modal/panel overlays, and reduced top-level real estate
facade.rejectImplies: Test full-width tab bars, flat navigation, breadcrumb-only wayfinding, and top-anchored command centers

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer tactile, continuous motion feedback over instant snaps when organizing workspace elements
facade.axisTargeted: Fluid gesture interaction vs instant click-based manipulation
facade.label: Drag to rearrange
facade.acceptImplies: Build drag-and-drop with smooth follow, momentum, and drop previews throughout the workspace
facade.rejectImplies: Use instant click-to-place, grid snapping, and modal dialogs for rearranging tasks or panels

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer a persistent left sidebar for core workspace functions paired with horizontal tabs for context-switching within sections
evidence.implication: Explore vertical hierarchies, persistent navigation, modal/panel overlays, and reduced top-level real estate

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI tools prefer dark interfaces that reduce eye strain during long coding sessions
facade.label: Dark workspace
facade.acceptImplies: Probe deeper into dark/night-mode preferences; test contrast levels and accent color brightness next
facade.rejectImplies: Explore light, high-contrast, or mid-tone interfaces; test whether users want energizing brightness or soft neutrals instead

facade.hypothesis: Users want to see multiple metrics at a glance in organized boxes rather than read flowing prose about their workspace status
facade.axisTargeted: Information density and layout—charts/grids vs narrative blocks
facade.label: Dashboard grid
facade.acceptImplies: Design favors scannable tile-based layouts with visual hierarchy; metrics and data organized spatially for quick parsing
facade.rejectImplies: Design favors prose, explanation, and narrative flow; information presented in connected paragraphs rather than isolated visual units

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces lean toward warm, organic lighting and subtle depth cues rather than harsh, clinical contrast
facade.label: Soft shadows
facade.acceptImplies: Explore layered, diffused interfaces with gentle gradients and ambient lighting; move away from flat, stark UI
facade.rejectImplies: Test crisp, minimal interfaces with high contrast and clean separation; consider cooler palettes and decisive edges

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface
Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication
Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simplified summaries and key metrics
Whether user likes smooth/gradual transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal/clean aesthetic vs rich/detailed visual design for their workspace
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs hidden/revealed on demand in their interface
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging/motivational tone vs neutral/professional communication
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants a calm/focused atmosphere vs energetic/stimulating workspace mood
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers data-heavy dashboards vs simplified summaries and key metrics
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user likes smooth/gradual transitions vs instant/snappy interface responses
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer an AI workspace that feels like talking to a helpful colleague rather than using a formal system
facade.label: Conversational tone
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into warm, approachable language; use 'we', casual phrasing, and humor where appropriate
facade.rejectImplies: Maintain formal, precise, distanced tone; use technical language and official documentation style

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer vertically stacked, collapsible content panels over tabbed interfaces within the main workspace, reducing cognitive load from tab switching and allowing multiple contexts visible simultaneously
facade.axisTargeted: Information architecture and visibility: monolithic vs modular, sequential vs parallel context access
facade.label: Stacked panels
facade.acceptImplies: Explore accordion patterns, progressive disclosure through collapse/expand, vertical scrolling as primary navigation, multiple simultaneous open states
facade.rejectImplies: Tabs remain superior for context isolation; users want clear separation between modes rather than overlapping information density

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI-assisted tasks, projects, and conversations in one unified dashboard.
